frameworks.name: terror management theory
frameworks.one_line: Mortality awareness drives compensatory psychological coping responses

frameworks.name: trait self-control
frameworks.one_line: Individual differences in self-regulation moderate affective responses

frameworks.name: attentional positivity bias
frameworks.one_line: Selective attention to positive over negative affective stimuli

core_question: Does trait self-control moderate the effect of mortality salience on attention to positive versus negative images?
key_assumptions: Gaze patterns index selective attentional allocation
Mortality salience can be experimentally induced via contemplation prompts
Trait self-control captures stable individual differences in regulation
Affective image valence reliably divides positive from negative stimuli
manipulations: Mortality salience versus control topic contemplation
Continuous trait self-control measured by questionnaire
Positive versus negative affective images presented during gaze recording
key_findings: Mortality salience increased attention to positive over negative images among high self-control individuals
Lower self-control individuals showed a non-significant trend in the opposite direction
A self-control by mortality salience interaction emerged on positivity bias in gaze
interpretations: High trait self-control facilitates a positivity bias as a coping response to death awareness
Attentional positivity bias may explain why self-controlled individuals attain better life outcomes
